A lot of people pull their economic punches because they're not liked. Wherever you went, our conversation on the break, a lot of the breaks we had a conversation with one of the IBAs who was encountered somebody who, and she went upset about it by the way, so don't misunderstand, but somebody who she told them, she used the Kennedy name and the great reaction she got to this powerful brand that we've developed is her prospect, oh I know the Kennedy thing and I think they're slimy. And I said, well, I think it'll be the last time you're going to hear it. I mean, because the aggressiveness causes that reaction from some people. And so the old Willie Lohman syndrome is, want to be liked, right? This is Donnie Deutch's quote, you know, it's a constant. Throughout my life I've always had people who don't know me, say from a distance, I really don't like that guy. In the NSA market where I did a lot of work, I used to get it all the time. And I would gradually hear the same thing he heard at the end, you know, somebody wind up doing this and you know, for years. I never met you, I didn't really know what you were buying like you. Well, do you worry about that or do you not worry about that? Well, money doesn't move to you because you are liked. Money doesn't not move to you because you are disliked by certain people. Money moves to you for other forces. There's lots of reasons why you won't be liked if you focus on making a lot of money in business. So here's one, new kid on the block after we've been laboring at this for years. By the way, there's a version of this in politics right now. If anybody thinks that Jesse Jackson and Al Sharpton are jumping up and down with joy over Obama, not happenin'. A bunch of folks aren't happy that they've been laboring for the last 30 years and this kid has come out of nowhere and actually might even have a shot at the crown. They ain't his biggest fans. Not happy. Who does he think he is anyway? And how dare he get to where they want to be faster but not pay your dues. Well, those are all reasons why you won't be liked. The more visible you are, the more successful you are, the faster you move, the more speed you create, the more money you attract to you, the more you will get this from some people. Here's what's really important to know about it. You're getting it from people who wouldn't give you money anyway. So what? They don't matter. Big thing culturally is charging for what others give away. How dare you do that? Right? There's a cultural attitude in some associations, for example, that you may belong to where you're because you're all members of the same thing, you're supposed to share and everybody's supposed to give everybody something for it. All their wisdom expertise, everything for free because we're all brothers in the same, you know. And so there's resentment when you figure out how to make money from it. If you spit on cherished traditions or icons, which you probably will have to in order to If you say provocative, critical or offensive things, which is a great way to attract attention, in fact, money to you, if you make demands on money, just making a lot of money. Lots of reasons for a lot of people not to like you. And a lot of people worry about all that. But the truth of the matter is everybody doesn't like you for all of those reasons, are people who would not be giving you money anyway. And so what? Your opinions do not count. The greatest attractant of money that I know of is immunity to criticism. There's a great Abraham Maslow quote. I'm sure you all know Maslow, here it is. High achievers leave independent of the good opinion of others. Now remember, we're in the business of dispensing gold stars. That's a business we're in. Good news is it's cheap currency because you get to print it legally. We're in the business of dispensing gold stars to others who need them from us in order to feel good about themselves. It's very important not to be one of them, but to be the superior being who does not need gold stars from other people. In order to feel good about ourselves.
